headline: CME Group and Morningstar Announce Exclusive Index Derivatives Licensing Agreement
summary: CME Group, the world's leading derivatives marketplace, along with Morningstar, a leading provider of independent investment insights, today announced that they have entered into a multi-year licensing agreement for CME Group to launch derivatives products based on key Morningstar equity index benchmarks, including the Morningstar US Total Market, Large Cap, Large Cap Value, Large Cap Growth, Mid Cap, and Small Cap Indexes.

headline: Get Ready for the Stock Market’s Volatile Summer
summary: Inflation, oil prices, Fed-rate concerns, Middle East tensions, and a wave of high-profile IPOs are creating the conditions for a volatile summer in stocks.

headline: BGC Group Stock: How Fenics and FMX Are Shifting the Mix
summary: BGC is leaning on brokerage, but Fenics' record Q1 and FMX's surging Treasury and FX volumes show its electronic push gaining traction.

headline: BGC Stock: Buyback Capacity vs. Expense Pressures in 2026
summary: BGC Global ends Q1 2026 with $878.4M in liquidity and $386.9M left on its $400M buyback, keeping repurchases in play even as expenses keep climbing.

headline: BGC Global and FMX: A Play on Electronification in U.S. Rates
summary: BGC is leaning on FMX and Fenics as electronification lifts U.S. rates and FX workflows, with share gains and new futures products widening the platform story.

headline: Core CPI Inflation Eases Fed Fears As Warsh Takes Helm (Live Coverage)
summary: The consumer price index showed inflation surging to a three-year high in May, but a moderate rise in core prices eased rate-hike fears on Wall Street.  Today's data and tomorrow's producer price index will influence the Federal Reserve policy stance that will be sketched out a week from today as Kevin Warsh presides over his first Fed meeting.  S&P 500 futures pared sharp overnight losses after the CPI inflation data calmed nervousness that the AI-fueled bull market is at risk of a time-out amid uncertain U.S.-Iran ceasefire negotiations and a potential hawkish shift by the Fed.
